title: rivina laevis var. acuminata
intro: Rivina laevis var. acuminata, also known as the West Indian sorrel or red mallow, is a plant species belonging to the family Phytolaccaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, treating wounds and skin irritations, and supporting digestive health. Rivina laevis var. acuminata is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its juice is also used in herbal remedies, making it a valuable plant in folk medicine.
